name: Aswanth Bindu Ajith
current_role: Master's Student in High Performance Computing and Quantum Computing
professional_summary: Interdisciplinary M.Sc. candidate bridging mechanical engineering fundamentals with advanced computational methods. Built Python-based simulations handling 30,000+ entities, optimized code workflows cutting runtime by 40%, and shipped production features in an Agile team. Comfortable across the stack: from numerical algorithms and GPU-aware parallel programming to frontend debugging and Git-based CI pipelines. Seeking roles where engineering rigor and code meet.

education.degree: M.Sc. High Performance Computing and Quantum Computing

education.institution: APJ Abdul Kalam Technological University, India

experience.title: Working Student Software Developer
description: Shipped 5+ frontend features in a 2-week sprint cycle, reducing average ticket age by 30%
Debugged and resolved 20+ production bugs across the React/Vue stack, cutting reported error rates by 25%
Wrote unit tests for 3 core modules, pushing test coverage from 45% to 78%
Collaborated with 4-person cross-functional team (design, backend, QA) using GitHub Flow and daily standups
Migrated 150+ product listings to a new API schema with zero downtime during deployment

projects.name: Large-Scale Computational Simulation & Data Analysis
description: Built a Python simulation engine processing 30,000+ interacting entities using vectorized NumPy operations
Reduced runtime from 12 minutes to 7 minutes (40% speedup) by replacing nested loops with Pandas groupby and broadcasting
Identified 3 non-obvious data correlations that shifted the team's modeling assumptions
Packaged results into an 18-page LaTeX report with reproducible Jupyter notebooks for peer review

projects.name: Computing Architecture & Optimization Methods
description: Implemented gradient descent and genetic algorithms from scratch to optimize a 50-dimensional engineering cost function
Benchmarked CPU vs. GPU (CUDA) performance on matrix operations, documenting 8x speedup for large matrices
Profiled memory bottlenecks using Python's cProfile and reduced peak RAM usage by 35% through generator-based streaming
Presented findings to a 25-person seminar, receiving top marks on the technical report

projects.name: Thermal Analysis of Battery Modules
description: Modeled heat transfer across a 12-cell lithium-ion battery pack using finite difference methods in Python
Simulated 8 operating scenarios, identifying a thermal runaway risk at >45°C under sustained 3C discharge
Proposed a revised cooling channel geometry that improved peak temperature uniformity by 18% in simulation
Co-authored a technical report cited by 2 subsequent student projects in the same lab

key_achievements: Reduced simulation runtime by 40% via vectorization on a 30,000-entity dataset
